seoTitle: How to Use Power Automate AI Copilot
metaDescription: See how Power Automate's AI Copilot builds, edits, and troubleshoots flows from natural language prompts, no advanced technical skills required.
intro: Building automation from scratch usually means learning triggers, actions, and expressions one at a time. Power Automate's AI Copilot changes that by letting you describe what you want in plain language. This guide walks through creating, editing, and fixing flows using Copilot so you can automate tasks faster.

steps.heading: What is Power Automate AI Copilot
steps.body: AI Copilot is a natural-language assistant built into Power Automate that can generate flows, explain existing logic, and suggest fixes without you writing each step manually.

steps.heading: Create a flow with AI Copilot
steps.body: Describe the workflow you want in a prompt, and Copilot proposes triggers and actions that match your description, which you can then review and adjust.

steps.heading: Edit and optimize existing flows
steps.body: Ask Copilot to modify a flow you already built, such as adding a condition or changing a connector, instead of editing each step by hand.

steps.heading: Troubleshoot and improve workflows with AI
steps.body: When a flow fails or behaves unexpectedly, Copilot can help explain the run history and suggest changes to resolve common errors.

faq.question: Do I need coding experience to use AI Copilot?
faq.answer: No, Copilot is designed for natural language prompts, so it's aimed at reducing the technical skill needed to build flows.

faq.question: Can AI Copilot connect to apps like Excel, Outlook, and Teams?
faq.answer: Yes, Copilot works alongside the standard Power Platform connectors, including Microsoft 365 apps like Excel, Outlook, and Teams.

faq.question: Will Copilot fix every error automatically?
faq.answer: Copilot can suggest fixes for common issues, but you should still review and test any changes it proposes before trusting the flow in production.

faq.question: Is AI Copilot only for beginners?
faq.answer: It's useful for beginners getting started, but developers and citizen developers can also use it to speed up building and editing more complex flows.
